{{Short description="Radiant energy emitted by charged particles undergoing acceleration}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Electromagnetic radiation
| image = Onde electromagnétique.png
| caption = An [[electromagnetic wave]], showing oscillating perpendicular [[electric field|electric]] and [[magnetic field]]s.
| topics = [[Electromagnetic wave]], [[Photon]], [[Electromagnetic spectrum]], [[Maxwell's equations]]
| speed = [[Speed of light]] (c) in vacuum
}}

'''Electromagnetic radiation''' ('''EM radiation''' or '''EMR''') is [[radiant energy]] released by certain electromagnetic processes. It consists of [[electromagnetic wave]]s, which are synchronized oscillations of [[electric field|electric]] and [[magnetic field]]s that propagate at the [[speed of light]] through vacuum.

Classically, EM radiation consists of electromagnetic waves. Quantum mechanically, it consists of discrete packets of energy called [[photon]]s, with energy proportional to [[frequency]]: <math>E = h\nu</math>.

== Maxwell's equations ==
{{Main|Maxwell's equations}}
EM radiation is predicted by [[Maxwell's equations]]. The wave equation for the electric field in vacuum is:

:<math>\nabla^2\mathbf{E} = \mu_0\varepsilon_0\frac{\partial^2\mathbf{E}}{\partial t^2}</math>

This reveals that <math>c = 1/\sqrt{\mu_0\varepsilon_0}</math> — the speed of light emerges from the constants of electromagnetism.

== Wave properties ==
EM waves are [[transverse wave]]s: the <math>\mathbf{E}</math> and <math>\mathbf{B}</math> fields are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of propagation. For a plane wave travelling in the +z-direction:

:<math>\mathbf{E} = E_0\cos(kz - \omega t)\hat{\mathbf{x}}</math>
:<math>\mathbf{B} = B_0\cos(kz - \omega t)\hat{\mathbf{y}}</math>
:<math>B_0 = E_0/c</math>

== Emission mechanisms ==
EM radiation is generated when [[electric charge|charged particles]] are accelerated:
* '''[[Bremsstrahlung]]''' — deceleration of charged particles in matter
* '''[[Black-body radiation]]''' — thermal motion of charges in matter
* '''[[Synchrotron radiation]]''' — relativistic charged particles in magnetic fields
* '''[[Cherenkov radiation]]''' — particles exceeding the local speed of light in a medium
* '''[[Atomic transition]]s''' — electrons transitioning between energy levels
* '''[[Nuclear transition]]s''' — gamma ray emission from excited nuclei
* '''[[Annihilation]]''' — particle–antiparticle collision

== Interactions with matter ==
* [[Photoelectric effect]]
* [[Compton scattering]]
* [[Pair production]]
* [[Rayleigh scattering]]
* [[Absorption (electromagnetic radiation)|Absorption]]

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electromagnetic spectrum]]
* [[Electromagnetic wave]]
* [[Photon]]
* [[Polarization (waves)]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Electromagnetic radiation| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetism]]
